title: Bachelor's Thesis — Polyp Detection
description: Bachelor's thesis on detecting polyps in colonoscopy images with deep learning, using CycleGAN and SPADE to generate synthetic training data when real labels were scarce.
lead: Train Faster R-CNN, RetinaNet, and SSD detectors on colonoscopy data, augmented with synthetic images from CycleGAN and SPADE generative models. Hyperparameter search via Optuna & Ray Tune.
metaDescription: Bachelor's thesis on polyp detection in colonoscopy with deep learning, using CycleGAN and SPADE for synthetic data. Interactive PyTorch + Optuna demo.

title: Draculin
description: Health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision assistance, built in Flutter and Django as a BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist.
lead: Walk through Draculin: a Flutter + Django health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision assistance. BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist.
metaDescription: Flutter + Django health-tracking app with an AI chatbot and computer vision. BitsXLaMarató 2023 finalist — browse the UI and interactive flows.
aboutDescription: Draculin was developed for the BitsXLaMarató 2023 hackathon, where it reached the finals. The app uses a Google LLM-powered chatbot for health guidance and computer vision to analyze images for menstrual health tracking. The frontend is built with Flutter (supporting iOS, Android, and web), while the backend runs on Django with image inference capabilities.

title: BitsXLaMarató — Aorta
description: BitsXLaMarató 2022 winner: a tool to segment the aorta in ultrasound videos with Mask R-CNN and reconstruct it in 3D to help detect aneurysms.
lead: Run the aorta pipeline live: abdominal ultrasound → Mask R-CNN segmentation → 3D mesh reconstruction → aortic diameter estimation. Built for the BitsXLaMarató hackathon.
metaDescription: BitsXLaMarató 2022 winner: Mask R-CNN aorta segmentation and 3D reconstruction for aneurysm detection. Interactive PyTorch + OpenCV inference demo.

title: License Plate Detection
description: Computer vision project to locate and read license plates from car photos, originally built as a classical MATLAB pipeline.
lead: Detect and read license plates from car photos. Pick a sample or upload your own and watch the 3-stage pipeline — plate detection, character segmentation, OCR — run entirely in your browser.
metaDescription: Computer vision project to locate and read license plates, originally in MATLAB and ported to TypeScript. Runs entirely in the browser.
aboutDescription: Originally a MATLAB project for the Computer Vision course at FIB-UPC. This browser demo is a port of the original pipeline to JavaScript — it uses adaptive binarization, morphological filtering, connected component analysis, and template matching to detect license plates and read their characters, all running client-side with no external dependencies.

title: Programming Game Viewer
description: Data structures course project: a grid-based game where C++ bots compete as warriors and builders in a class-wide tournament.
lead: Watch C++ AI bots compete in "The Purge" — a grid-based strategy game from the Data Structures and Algorithms course at FIB-UPC.
metaDescription: Data structures course project: C++ AI bots competing on a grid. Interactive Canvas replay viewer with bundled or live matches.
aboutDescription: Written in C++11, the game engine runs 4 AI players simultaneously on a 2D grid. The custom AI (AIJohnnyMelavo) uses BFS pathfinding to prioritize weapon collection, enemy hunting, and resource gathering. Each player controls warriors (fighters) and builders (constructors) who compete for money, food, guns, and bazookas across day/night cycles.

title: JSBach Interpreter
description: Compilers course project: JSBach, a small programming language where you describe musical pieces as code, built with an ANTLR4 grammar.
lead: Write music with code: JSBach is a small language with procedures, variables, loops, and conditionals that plays back musical notes. Try the examples or write your own.
metaDescription: Compilers course project: JSBach, a small music programming language built with ANTLR4. Write code and play it live in a Web Audio interpreter.
aboutDescription: Originally built with ANTLR4 and Python for a compilers course at FIB-UPC. The language supports procedures, recursion, arithmetic, lists, conditionals, loops, and musical note playback. This browser version uses a hand-written parser and the Web Audio API for sound synthesis.

title: Tenda Online
description: Web applications course project: a full e-commerce store built in PHP and MySQL, covering product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout flow.
lead: Browse an e-commerce store built in PHP and MySQL: explore products by category, add to cart, and walk through the checkout flow.
metaDescription: Web applications course project: full PHP + MySQL e-commerce store with catalog, cart, and checkout. Browse against the live backend or a static rebuild.
aboutDescription: A complete e-commerce platform with product catalog, category browsing, shopping cart, checkout flow, and an admin order management panel. Features include dynamic product pages, cart persistence, and order processing.

title: WPGMA Clustering
description: Programming course project: implementing WPGMA clustering in C++ to build phylogenetic trees from species gene sequences.
lead: Build phylogenetic trees step by step. Add species with gene sequences, inspect the k-mer distance matrix, and watch WPGMA merge the closest clusters into a dendrogram.
metaDescription: Programming course project: WPGMA clustering in C++ building phylogenetic trees from gene sequences. Interactive D3 dendrogram.
aboutDescription: Originally a C++17 project for the PRO2 course at FIB-UPC. The algorithm computes pairwise distances between species using k-mer frequency analysis, then iteratively merges the closest pair using Weighted Pair Group Method with Arithmetic Mean (WPGMA) to build a phylogenetic tree.

title: MPIDS Solver
description: Algorithms project on the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set problem, solved with greedy heuristics and simulated-annealing-style local search.
lead: Find the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set on a graph — the smallest set where every vertex has at least half its neighbors inside. Pick a sample or load your own, then run greedy or local search.
metaDescription: Algorithms project on the Minimum Positive Influence Dominating Set, solved with greedy and local-search heuristics. Interactive D3 force-directed graph editor.
aboutDescription: Originally a C++ project for the Algorithmics course at FIB-UPC, solving the MPIDS problem with greedy heuristics, simulated annealing, and CPLEX ILP. This full-stack web app reimplements the algorithms in Python (FastAPI backend) with an Elm + D3.js frontend providing an interactive force-directed graph visualization via ports.

title: Graph Phase Transitions
description: Algorithms research project studying connectivity and complexity phase transitions on binomial, geometric, and grid random graphs.
lead: Watch random graphs transition from connected to disconnected as you remove nodes or edges. Generate binomial (Erdős–Rényi), geometric, or grid graphs, apply percolation, and see the phase transition in real time.
metaDescription: Algorithms research on connectivity and complexity phase transitions in binomial, geometric, and grid random graphs. Interactive percolation animations and Monte Carlo charts.
aboutDescription: Originally a Python project for the Algorithmics course at FIB-UPC, studying phase transitions in random graphs using NetworkX. This full-stack web app wraps the original Python/NetworkX logic in a FastAPI backend with a Lit (Web Components) + Canvas + D3.js frontend for interactive graph visualization and Monte Carlo phase transition charts.

title: Travel agency (PDDL)
description: Automated planning course project: a PDDL domain for booking trips with flights, hotels, numeric constraints, and metric minimization.
lead: Browse a PDDL travel-agency domain — flights, hotels, stay duration, and a numeric interest metric to minimize. Inspect the Extension 2 files, download .pddl sources, or run the planner when an ENHSP backend is available.
metaDescription: Automated planning course project: a PDDL travel-agency domain with flights, hotels, numeric constraints, and metric minimization. Interactive planner demo.

title: Desastres IA
description: AI course project framing disaster relief as local search, with helicopters rescuing groups from rescue centers via hill climbing and simulated annealing.
lead: Solve disaster-relief assignment as local search — helicopters rescuing groups from centers on an interactive 2D map. Compare hill climbing vs simulated annealing with 3 heuristics and 6 successor functions.
metaDescription: AI course project framing disaster relief as local search: helicopters rescuing groups via hill climbing and simulated annealing. Solid.js + FastAPI interactive solver.

title: Machine Learning — Hypothyroid
description: Machine learning course practica: training and comparing nine sklearn model families to predict hypothyroidism from clinical data.
lead: Explore an end-to-end hypothyroidism classification pipeline with 9 sklearn model families. The interactive demos below run real trained models directly in your browser.
metaDescription: Machine learning course practica comparing 9 sklearn model families to predict hypothyroidism. Interactive notebook, live predictor, and k-NN PCA classifier.

title: Recommendation System
description: Software engineering course project: a content-based recommendation system built in Java with Spring Boot and Thymeleaf, including a full web interface.
lead: Browse a content-based recommendation system built in Java with Spring Boot and Thymeleaf — modelling users, items, and ratings to deliver personalized picks.
metaDescription: Software engineering course project: a Java Spring Boot content-based recommender with a Thymeleaf web UI. Browse the flow live or as a static mockup.
aboutDescription: A university project for the "Projectes de Programació" course at FIB-UPC. The core domain logic is written in pure Java and features complex data structures and algorithms for filtering and recommending items based on past user ratings and item attributes (K-Nearest Neighbors and Collaborative Filtering concepts). The web interface is powered by Spring Boot and server-side rendered with Thymeleaf and Bootstrap 5.

title: CAIM IR Explorer
description: Information retrieval course labs: computing PageRank over the global airport network and verifying Zipf's Law on text corpora.
lead: Explore core information retrieval ideas interactively: run PageRank on the global airport network with a world-map visualization, and fit Zipf's Law on real text corpora.
metaDescription: Information retrieval course labs: PageRank on the global airport network and Zipf's Law on text corpora. Interactive D3 world map and curve-fitting tool.
aboutDescription: Originally lab exercises for the CAIM (Search and Analysis of Information) course at FIB-UPC, covering PageRank, Zipf/Heap laws, TF-IDF, Rocchio, MapReduce, and graph analysis. This full-stack web app reimplements the PageRank and Zipf algorithms in Python (FastAPI) with a Vanilla TypeScript + D3.js frontend — no UI framework, just plain TypeScript managing the DOM directly.

title: Trip Planner (Expert System)
description: Knowledge-based systems course project: an expert system trip planner originally written with CLIPS production rules, reimplemented in Python.
lead: Answer 10 questions on trip type, budget, duration, and hotel preferences, and get a full itinerary with cities, hotels, activities, transport, and cost. A CLIPS expert system reimplemented in Python with an HTMX + Alpine.js frontend.
badge: Knowledge-Based Systems · HTMX + Alpine.js + FastAPI · FIB-UPC
metaDescription: Knowledge-based systems course project: a CLIPS-style expert system trip planner reimplemented in Python with an HTMX + Alpine.js frontend.

title: Parallel Computing Labs
description: Parallel computing course labs in C with OpenMP: Mandelbrot, heat equation, 4-way merge sort, π estimation, and speedup benchmarks.
lead: Run five OpenMP labs compiled to WebAssembly: the Mandelbrot set, the heat equation with Jacobi iteration, 4-way merge-sort, numerical π, and parallel speedup benchmarks.
badge: Parallel Computing · Preact + Canvas + WASM · FIB-UPC
metaDescription: Parallel computing course labs in C with OpenMP — Mandelbrot, heat equation, 4-way merge sort, π, and speedup — compiled to WebAssembly in-browser.

title: Robotics Dashboard
description: Robotics course labs covering odometry, wall-following, 3D arm forward kinematics (Puma560 / 3-Link), trajectory planning, sensors, and EKF state estimation.
lead: Step through six interactive robotics visualizations built with Ember.js and Babylon.js: mobile-robot odometry with laser scans, wall-following, 3D arm kinematics (Puma560/3-Link), trajectory planning, sensor dashboards, and EKF state estimation.
metaDescription: Robotics course labs: odometry, wall-following, 3D arm kinematics, trajectory planning, sensors, and EKF. Interactive Ember.js + Babylon.js dashboard.

title: Algorithm Visualizer
description: Algorithms and data structures coursework covering 18 classical algorithms across graphs, sorting, search, and combinatorial problems.
lead: Step through 18 classic algorithms — Dijkstra, Prim's MST, BFS/DFS, sorting, N-Queens, 2-opt TSP, and more — with interactive visualizations built in Qwik and Canvas.
metaDescription: Algorithms and data structures coursework — 18 classical algorithms across graphs, sorting, search, and combinatorics. Interactive Qwik + Canvas visualizer.

title: Computer Graphics Shader Playground
description: Computer graphics course labs originally written against desktop OpenGL, covering vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, fragment patterns, and geometry shader emulation.
lead: Edit WebGL2 shaders live — vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, fragment patterns, geometry effects, and more. Ports the FIB-UPC Gràfics course labs to the browser.
metaDescription: Computer graphics course labs originally for desktop OpenGL, covering vertex transforms, Phong lighting, procedural textures, and geometry effects. Live WebGL2 shader playground.
